CC 2.1.203 + 2.1.204 Adoption

38 changelog bullets triaged 5 gap issues resolved 4 skill files edited latest_known 2.1.202 → 2.1.204 floor frozen 2.1.183 Risk LOW
THE REAL ADOPTION — #2785

Worktree-isolation accuracy repair

Three ork files claim CC 2.1.154 fully closed the worktree-escape bug. It didn't — a residual leak let isolated subagents run shell commands in the parent checkout through 2.1.202. Since ork's floor is 2.1.183, the claim was false for the entire supported range. CC 2.1.203 completes the fix.

Ork claimed (inaccurate for 2.1.183–2.1.202) "subagents … no longer bypass the worktree-isolation guard" — attributed wholly to 2.1.154 in version-compatibility.md:382, worktree-agent-pattern.md:73, manual-worktree-pattern.md
Corrected (2.1.203) 2.1.154 added the isolation guard; a residual Bash/git-in-parent-checkout leak persisted until 2.1.203. All three files updated + new feature-matrix row.

[1]Verdict board — 5 gap issues + featureless 2.1.204

ADOPT

Worktree isolation leak

#2785 · 2.1.203

Accuracy repair across 3 files (see spotlight). Rest of the worktree cluster (nested repos, non-git dirs, arg-list) — no ork text, pure upstream fixes.

ADOPT · narrow

Startup warnings → /doctor + /status

#2787 · 2.1.203

Two feature-matrix rows (warning relocation + LSP-disuse fix). No skill-logic change — ork's doctor validates hook commands independently.

ADVISORY NOTE

Re-delegation reduced

#2786 · 2.1.203

Ork has no anti-re-delegation guard to soften — Pattern 9 enables nested delegation. One CC-version-trail paragraph added to chain-patterns Pattern 9.

NO-OP

ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL leak fix

#2784 · 2.1.203

All 3 flagged skills were keyword collisions (401, gateway, LiteLLM). Ork never documented the buggy drop; snapshot already captured by cc-watch.

INFO ROW

MCP roots/list_changed

#2788 · 2.1.203

Client-side capability CC advertises to MCP servers; ork consumes no MCP roots. One informational feature-matrix row.

INFO ROW

SessionStart headless streaming

2.1.204 · featureless

Upstream fix; ork's 4 SessionStart hooks are already fast + async with a 180s idle watchdog. One feature-matrix row anchors the triage.

[2]Auto-filer precision — skill claims per issue

#2784
3 / 3 false
#2785
3 / 4 false
#2786
3 / 5 false
#2787
0 / 3 false*
#2788
3 / 3 false

9 of 14 distinct skill claims across the 5 auto-filed issues were keyword matches (isolation, 401, capabilities, MCP) with no real edit surface. *#2787 named the right skill family but overstated behavioral impact (docs-only). Recorded for cc-triage prompt tuning.

[3]Edit manifest

  • Msrc/skills/doctor/references/version-compatibility.md+5 matrix rows · soften :382 over-claim
  • Msrc/skills/chain-patterns/references/worktree-agent-pattern.mdresidual-leak note at :73
  • Msrc/skills/implement/references/manual-worktree-pattern.mdSUPERSEDED banner: fix completed in 2.1.203
  • Msrc/skills/chain-patterns/SKILL.mdPattern 9 CC-version-trail paragraph
  • Mshared/cc-support.jsonlatest_known 2.1.202 → 2.1.204 + override note
  • Adocs/audits/cc-adoption-2.1.203-204-triage-2026-07-08.mdfull 38-bullet triage record

[4]Execution pipeline

Edits4 skills + support json → Stampstamp-cc-support.mjs → Buildnpm run build → Testnpm test → Triage docdocs/audits/ → PRcloses #2784–#2788 + #2776

No cc-adoption-gaps.json edit needed — both entries carry issues_filed_at, so the next cc-watch run auto-prunes them.

Adopt CC 2.1.203+2.1.204 in orchestkit: repair the three worktree-isolation over-claims (real fix is 2.1.203, not 2.1.154), add the five feature-matrix rows, note re-delegation in Pattern 9, bump latest_known to 2.1.204, then build, test, and open one bundled PR closing #2784-#2788 and #2776.
